# ARC-QUIC-RFC9002-0004 - QUIC RFC 9002 PTO Timer Discipline Architecture

## Purpose

Describe the runtime-owned recovery seam that arms PTO, restarts its backoff, and applies validation-aware client and server gating while keeping PTO expiration separate from loss declaration.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2-0003
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P1-0006
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P1-0008
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P1-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P2P1-0001
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P2P1-0002
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P2P1-0003
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P2P1-0004
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P2P1-0005
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P2P2P1-0006

## Design Summary

The runtime keeps PTO discipline in the packet-space recovery ledger instead of introducing a second timer model. PTO arming uses the helper RTT and ack-delay state, restart and backoff-reset events flow through the send/recovery seam on ack-eliciting transmission, acknowledgments, and key discard, and pre-confirmation client and server gating consult actual validation state before application PTO is armed. PTO expiry advances scheduler state toward probe work but does not itself mark packets lost.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionSendRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicRecoveryTiming.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicPathValidation.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicHandshakeFlowCoordinator.cs

## Risks

- PTO expiry must stay scheduler state only so the timer transition cannot overclaim packet loss.
- Validation-aware gating must remain tied to real handshake and path-validation state, not to test-only assumptions.
- Backoff reset must stay synchronized with send, acknowledgment, and key-discard events so the PTO floor does not drift from packet-space state.
